title: Most Western Golf Brands Are Buying Impressions. None of Them Are Building Identity

text_hierarchy.text: Most Western Golf Brands Are Buying Impressions. None of Them Are Building Identity

Most golf brands today focus only on golf green storytelling, with limited creator diversity and highly repetitive content. This fails to build consumer connection with the brand and further reinforces stereotypes around golf culture.

images.description: Xiaohongshu (RED) post screenshot by creator 'Tianchi' showing a female golfer in a green-and-white striped golf dress and white visor carrying a HONMA golf bag on a fairway; caption mentions HONMA BERES.
Xiaohongshu post screenshot ('golf dayyy') of a woman in a pink G/Fore polo and pink visor standing beside a golf cart on a green course with hills behind; representing the G/Fore brand.
Xiaohongshu post screenshot by creator '小猴子' showing a woman in a white-and-black golf outfit crouching beside a white J.LINDEBERG golf bag on a course; caption references J.Lindeberg ACE.
Xiaohongshu post screenshot ('Tianchi', 'Preppy drop') of a woman in a white FILA Golf polo and pleated white skirt holding a club beside a golf bag and cart; represents FILA Golf.
Xiaohongshu post screenshot of a man in a white polo and navy jacket seated on a vintage green golf cart in sunlight; caption 'Preppy life continues.' representing Ralph Lauren golf styling.

logical_links: The bold title states the core argument: rival Western golf brands buy impressions but fail to build a distinct identity. Beneath it, five near-identical phone screenshots of Xiaohongshu creator posts are arranged in a horizontal row, each labeled with a competitor name (HONMA, G/Fore, J.Lindberg, FILA Golf, Ralph Lauren). Their visual sameness — golfers in preppy attire posed on the green or by a cart — is itself the evidence: the bottom body paragraph explains that these brands only do green-focused storytelling with limited creator diversity and repetitive content, which reinforces golf stereotypes rather than connecting with consumers. The slide reads top-to-bottom: claim, then a gallery proving the claim, then the diagnosis.

title: Break Out of the Golf Bubble

text_hierarchy.text: Break Out of the Golf Bubble

children.text: Cooperated Creators should not function only as clothing models, but as expressions of the brand’s style and identity, helping audiences form the right associations with the brand.

text_hierarchy.text: Maintain golf culture storytelling while moving beyond traditional green-focused narratives and overly commercial creator content. Reframe golf culture through a more connected, relaxed, and modern storytelling approach.

images.description: Two young women laughing together inside a golf cart, wearing matching green-and-white checkered/striped golf outfits and a checkered beanie; relaxed, candid lifestyle moment on a course.
A group of young hikers in an evergreen forest at golden hour, smiling; outdoorsy, friends-in-nature lifestyle scene unrelated to a golf green.
A woman in a yellow polo and skirt crouching on a red clay tennis court holding a racquet, with a tennis ball nearby; sporty editorial style outside golf.
A child/young person in a black hoodie, blue cap and shorts walking a Collie dog across a city crosswalk; urban streetwear lifestyle moment.
A skateboarder in an orange top and cap captured mid-motion riding along a sunlit concrete wall/pavement; dynamic urban skate scene.
A young man with curly hair in a black tee and shorts riding a chrome BMX/mini bike across a crosswalk; street cycling lifestyle.
A person in a black tee and red cap lying playfully against the hood of a silver sports car, arms spread; playful automotive/street lifestyle shot.

logical_links: The title 'Break Out of the Golf Bubble' frames the recommendation, and its subtitle clarifies that creators should embody the brand's style and identity rather than act as mere clothing models. The surrounding photo grid visually demonstrates the breakout: the leftmost large image keeps golf culture present (two friends in a cart), while the cluster on the right deliberately spans adjacent worlds — hiking, tennis, dog-walking, skateboarding, BMX, and car culture — showing the 'connected, relaxed, modern' storytelling the bottom callout prescribes. The images thus prove the thesis that the brand can stay rooted in golf while expanding into broader lifestyle contexts; the bold bottom callout restates the balance to strike between maintaining golf storytelling and escaping green-only, overly commercial content.

title: From Influencers to Brand Insiders — Building a Creator Community

text_hierarchy.text: From Influencers to Brand Insiders — Building a Creator Community
One campaign buys attention. A community builds a brand.
Paid Campaign — amplify what's already real
Offline Events — turn followers into a circle
Here's what this looks like when it compounds over time

children.text: By the end of 2025, we had help Marimekko developed over 50 high-potential creators for long-term partnerships.
Creators with partnerships lasting over one year tend to deliver stronger sales performance during key promotional periods.

images.description: Creator @XY_M posing outside a pink storefront in a white outfit with a green top, casual street-style; early seeding-stage content.
The same creator wearing a blue patterned Marimekko co-ord set, standing in a stone/courtyard setting; more polished branded campaign look.
Creator in a white top and long orange-and-white floral Marimekko maxi skirt standing in a green garden; branded fashion editorial.
Group/try-on style shot of a woman holding up a brown patterned knit and bag with smaller inset images of others; styling/haul content.
Creator @麦颗粒 in a black-and-white floral midi dress walking on a tree-lined city sidewalk; relaxed lifestyle look.
Four-panel 'AUTUMN OUTFITS' collage of the creator modeling several autumn looks (brown jacket, dark dress, cream sweater); seasonal lookbook content.
Three people posing in front of a large green-and-white Marimekko Unikko floral backdrop, likely at a brand event/fashion show; community/offline moment.
Creator in a pink top and blue floral Marimekko skirt posing by a green fountain in a park; high-production branded content.

logical_links: The title argues for converting influencers into 'brand insiders' via a community, and the subtitle sharpens the thesis: a single campaign buys attention while a community builds the brand. Three column headings near the top lay out the funnel — Seeding (identify the brand lover), Paid Campaign (amplify what's already real), and Offline Events (turn followers into a circle). The body block introduces the Marimekko Creator Program as proof, citing 50+ long-term creators and the claim that 1-year+ partnerships sell better in key periods. The two creator journeys below are timelines: @XY_M (seeding 2024 → 618 2024 → D11 2025, GMV 400k+) and @麦颗粒 (seeding 2023 → D11 2023 → Tokyo fashion show 2024 → D11 2025, GMV 200k+), each photo row showing a creator's content maturing from casual seeding shots to polished branded and event imagery. The bottom-left 'How does it work' tab marks this as part of the methodology section.

title: We have a powerful fashion KOL network for in-depth collaboration

text_hierarchy.text: We have a powerful fashion KOL network for in-depth collaboration

children.text: Over 5,670 KOL for premium/high fashion industry
Detail profile including the style, promotion value, and e-commerce conversion capability
Strong personal connection with over 1,500 influencers for in-depth collaboration
Tracking beyond social indicators: Mini Program visits, Tmall search traffic, add to cart
Deep collaboration with media buy team to maxims impact of campaign
Provide customized optimization suggestions including additional sharing within KOL’s private groups

images.description: Full-height screenshot of the WalktheChat 'Social Listening Dashboard' on a dark purple UI: a brand search bar, KPI tiles (Analyzed articles 2,328; Analyzed comments 99,427; Positive comments 65%; Verified influencers 502), a Pros and Cons section, a list of influencers talking about the brand with fan counts, audience stats (Top tiers Cities 65%, Active followers 50%, Part of women 95%, More than 25 years old 46%), and a 'Most commented articles' feed.

logical_links: The bold title asserts the agency's core capability — a powerful fashion KOL network for in-depth collaboration. The left text column substantiates this with two evidence blocks: a 'Fashion focused KOL database' (5,670+ premium-fashion KOLs, detailed profiles, 1,500+ deep relationships) and a 'Scientific campaign monitor' (tracking Mini Program visits, Tmall search, add-to-cart, media-buy coordination, and customized optimization). The full-bleed dashboard screenshot on the right is the proof point, visually demonstrating those claims in action — analyzed articles/comments, verified influencers, audience demographics, and per-influencer fan counts — so the imagery operationalizes the abstract bullet points. The 'How we select' tab anchors the slide in the selection-methodology section, and '30' is the page number.

title: We structure creator partnerships in tiers — matching investment level to audience scale and content role

text_hierarchy.text: We structure creator partnerships in tiers — matching investment level to audience scale and content role

Strong influence and credibility, helping shape brand perception at scale
Broad audience reach, driving awareness beyond the core golf community

Stronger engagement and audience trust
More authentic and relatable content
Higher content efficiency and consistency

Stable post performance
More cost-efficient for scaled campaigns
Higher chance to get a viral post
Support conversion through trusted recommendations

KOLs that are growing
Cost-efficient and generate good quality of content
More cooperative regarding the product selection

logical_links: The title establishes the organizing principle: creator partnerships are tiered so investment level matches audience scale and content role. A two-column table ('Cooperation Format' vs 'Strength') delivers this, with four rows that implicitly descend from top-tier to growing creators: top influencers used for Seeding/relationship-building (broad reach, credibility), then mid-tier creators across Seeding/Editorial/Offline (engagement, authentic content), then Seeding/Paid/Offline performers (stable, cost-efficient, conversion-driving), and finally smaller growing KOLs used purely for Seeding (cost-efficient, cooperative). Each row pairs the cooperation format with its distinct strength, so the reader can see why each tier earns a different role and budget. The 'How we select' tab places this within the selection-methodology section; the slide has no images, relying entirely on the table.

title: Continuously engage high-potential lifestyle audiences via in-feeds ads and efficiently expand beyond the core community through offline activations

text_hierarchy.text: Continuously engage high-potential lifestyle audiences via in-feeds ads and efficiently expand beyond the core community through offline activations

children.text: Continuously through targeted lifestyle audience, turning potential users into part of the Malbon community
Drive concentrated brand exposure in a short period of time through brand advertising via WeChat Moments and RED Branding Banner

logical_links: A small 'Advertising Strategy' label tops the slide, and the title states the dual goal: continuously engage high-potential lifestyle audiences via in-feed ads while expanding beyond the core community through offline activations. The body is split into two horizontal lanes that contrast in cadence and purpose. The 'Always-ON' lane is labeled 'Precise' and targets defined lifestyle segments (Sport Community, Style Trendsetter, Sports & Outdoor trendy enthusiasts), continuously converting potential users into the Malbon community. The 'KEY MOMENTS' lane is labeled 'Broaden Reach' and adds ('+') new audiences (Tourism & City Explorers, International & Business Professionals) plus Offline Events, driving concentrated short-burst exposure via WeChat Moments and RED Branding Banner. The two rows thus form an always-on (precision) versus burst (reach) comparison that together fulfills the title's engage-and-expand strategy; the slide is text/diagram only with no images.

title: Gain free exposure by seeding high-potential influencers, and amplify product visibility through reposts on official accounts supported by RED ads.

text_hierarchy.text: Gain free exposure by seeding high-potential influencers, and amplify product visibility through reposts on official accounts supported by RED ads.
Reposting on OA and Sales Account

images.description: Xiaohongshu profile/feed screenshot for a Marimekko-related official account showing tabs (商品/笔记/收藏/标签) and a grid of denim-themed posts ('DENIM ON DENIM', 'Home: 斑斓多彩') with like counts; illustrates reposting on an official account.
Xiaohongshu post screenshot ('Marimekko 三里屯店 Kiki') of two people in matching denim jackets and red Marimekko prints posing on a lawn; branded repost-style content with hashtags.
A man in a red-and-white striped Nike rugby shirt and blue trousers seated against a wall of sneaker/poster collage; streetwear fashion KOL look (@thefour style).
A woman in a graphic tee, cargo pants and beanie sitting on tiled steps with a skateboard and camera; skater/lifestyle KOL aesthetic (@琚琚琚橘子 style).
A woman in a white short dress and sporty socks/sneakers walking briskly past a red storefront window; clean fashion-lifestyle KOL look (@Leona_ style).

logical_links: The title lays out a two-step seeding mechanic: first gain free exposure by seeding high-potential influencers, then amplify product visibility by reposting on official/sales accounts supported by RED ads. The left two-thirds is the seeding step, headed 'High-potential Fashion and Lifestyle influencers' with the emphatic callout 'Don’t buy!!!' (i.e., earn organic placement rather than pay), and three creator photos each captioned with handle, fanbase, and niche — @Leona_ (170k+, golf), @琚琚琚橘子 (90k+, skaters), @thefour (38k+, outdoor) — spanning adjacent lifestyle communities. The right third is the amplification step: 'Reposting on OA and Sales Account' (to increase hero-product visibility and sales touchpoints), evidenced by two Xiaohongshu screenshots of official-account feeds and reposted branded content. Reading left-to-right mirrors the funnel from organic seeding to official-account amplification; the 'Seeding strategy' tab and page number 33 anchor the slide.

title: Based on the brand’s annual product launch plan, we recommend developing tailored, theme-based campaigns around key collection launches as important brand moments.

text_hierarchy.text: Based on the brand’s annual product launch plan, we recommend developing tailored, theme-based campaigns around key collection launches as important brand moments.

children.text: Localized global campaign strategy into China-specific creative direction, including concept development for official campaign assets such as product and model visuals
Designed integrated influencer and paid media strategies to support both brand storytelling and performance objectives
Enabled a full-funnel marketing approach, driving seamless conversion from awareness to purchase through coordinated content and media execution

images.description: MACKAGE official OASIS campaign screenshot: a man and woman in MACKAGE outerwear sitting on a park bench among autumn trees; China-localized brand asset.
MACKAGE official OASIS campaign screenshot: a couple in MACKAGE jackets walking down stone city steps with motion-blurred passersby; urban localized campaign visual.
A man in a bright blue MACKAGE jacket and grey trousers leaning against a brick wall with a poster behind; KOL urban-scene content.
Four-panel '风雨衣时髦穿搭 / CHARM'S RAINCOAT STYLES' lookbook collage of women modeling different MACKAGE raincoats/jackets; product-led outfit inspiration.
A woman in a cream/white MACKAGE jacket and beanie posing in front of an ornate carved wooden door on a city street; product-led styling shot.
A smiling woman in a black MACKAGE rain jacket and cap riding a bicycle in the rain, looking back over her shoulder; casual outdoor KOL scene.
A man in a black MACKAGE jacket, dark trousers and red cap walking on grass under trees; casual outdoor KOL content.
Four-panel 'Spring lookbook' collage of a woman modeling several MACKAGE spring jackets in black, cream, beige and green; product-led outfit inspiration.

logical_links: The bold title makes the recommendation — build tailored, theme-based campaigns around key collection launches as brand moments — and the rest of the slide proves it with the 'Case Study: MACKAGE 26SS OASIS Campaign'. The upper-left 'Context' block explains what was done (localizing the global campaign into China-specific creative, integrated influencer+paid media, a full-funnel awareness-to-purchase approach), while the two MACKAGE OASIS official campaign screenshots in the top right show the localized brand assets that anchored the theme. The lower band of KOL photos is grouped by the captions 'KOL - Casual outdoor setting', 'KOL- Urban scene', and 'KOL - Product-led outfit inspiration', demonstrating the diverse creator content produced under the campaign theme ('Localization Campaign Shooting'). The bottom-left 'Results' block closes the cause-effect loop with the payoff KPIs: 15,162 tracking UV of Tmall and an estimated total campaign ROI of 1.79.
